title: primula auriculata subsp. olgae
intro: Primula auriculata subsp. olgae, also known as Olg's Auricula, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the primrose family, native to Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Primula auriculata subsp. olgae include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe sore throats, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas or added to salves and ointments.
